headline: Cosmetic Surgery Complications Rise 62% With Unregulated Providers
context: Complications from cosmetic procedures performed by non-specialists increased sharply as the industry booms.

cards.big: Cosmetic surgery complications rose 62% from 2023 to 2025 with 8 deaths reported
cards.sub: Non-specialist doctors and unlicensed practitioners perform high-risk procedures

cards.big: 340 clinics offer cosmetic surgery but only 148 are operated by board-certified plastic surgeons
cards.sub: 52% of cosmetic surgery providers lack formal surgical specialization credentials

cards.big: Social media promotion of cosmetic procedures increased 280% on Malaysian platforms since 2022
cards.sub: Aggressive marketing normalizes surgery while minimizing risk information for consumers

cards.big: South Korea requires all cosmetic surgery advertising to include risk warnings since 2023
cards.sub: Mandatory risk disclosure reduced impulse cosmetic procedures by 18%

cards.big: An industry where half the providers lack proper credentials is not healthcare—it is gambling with bodies
Should only board-certified specialists be permitted to perform invasive cosmetic procedures
